summary:
The presence or absence of specific skills at three years can signify whether a child has reached expected developmental milestones; failure to hop on one foot may suggest delayed gross motor function among various potential indicators. Key here are notable gaps from established norms rather than isolated achievements. To directly answer regarding core criteria:

The critical aspect involves identifying clear deviations from normative growth patterns where missing out on more advanced but not strictly necessary tasks might highlight broader issues warranting further evaluation. Agent2 reasoned that while multiple abilities should ideally develop around this time frame, certain ones stand as markers due to their progression timeline within normal ranges. Hopping unilaterally was deemed particularly indicative because its emergence signifies maturation beyond simpler locomotor functions already presumed mastered earlier during childhood trajectory. Hence pinpointing such singular omission helps flag overall progress concerns effectively without overemphasizing minor variations common even amongst healthy individuals developing normally across different paces yet still tracking along general trajectories set forth through research into pediatric neuropsychological assessments conducted globally. 

In summary,
the lack of ability to hop on one foot stands out as an important signifier when assessing early signs of possible developmental lag since mastering bilateral coordination represents advancement past Key here are notable gaps from established norms rather than isolated achievements. To directly answer regarding core criteria:

Hopping unilaterally was deemed particularly indicative because its emergence signifies maturation beyond simpler locomotor functions already presumed mastered earlier during childhood trajectory.
